summary: Completed frontend testing of NASSAQ Platform - Iteration 18. All 5 requested features tested and PASSED: 1) Teacher Account Login & Dashboard ✓, 2) Welcome Card with Avatar and User Name ✓, 3) Cascading Filters (Region -> City) ✓, 4) PDF Export ✓, 5) Sparkline Charts ✓

issues: Console warning: 'The width(-1) and height(-1) of chart should be greater than 0' - Recharts library warning, does not affect functionality. Consider adding minWidth/minHeight to ResponsiveContainer.

passed_tests: - Dashboard shows: 5 classes, 125 students, 4 today's lessons, 2 pending attendance
- Welcome card shows 'Welcome, معلم تجريبي' with Avatar
- Quick Actions: تسجيل الحضور, إضافة تقييم, جدولي, الإشعارات
- Shows 'مرحباً أستاذ مدير المنصة' (Welcome Professor Platform Admin)
- Shows Avatar with user initial letter
- Selecting 'المنطقة الغربية' (Western Region) updates city dropdown
- City dropdown shows only Western cities: جدة، مكة المكرمة، المدينة المنورة، الطائف، ينبع
- Data changes based on filter: 210 schools -> 3 schools (Western Region)
- Export button shows dropdown with PDF and Excel options
- Success toast 'تم تنزيل ملف PDF بنجاح' displayed
- Found 8 area chart elements with gradient fill
- Colored trend indicator on left side (green=up, red=down)
critical_code_review_comments: Recharts console warning about chart dimensions - consider adding minWidth/minHeight to ResponsiveContainer to suppress warnings
context_for_next_testing_agent: All requested features tested successfully. Teacher dashboard at /teacher route works correctly with proper role-based access. Welcome card shows user name with 'أستاذ' prefix and Avatar. Cascading filters work: Region -> City dropdown updates dynamically. PDF export generates real PDF file using jsPDF. Sparkline charts visible with gradient fill and trend indicators.

mocked_apis.note: All data comes from real MongoDB database. Backend API /api/dashboard/stats supports region and city filter parameters.

pdf_export.generates: Real PDF file using jsPDF library

sparkline_charts.trend_indicator: Colored bar on left (green=up, red=down, gray=stable)
